verdict.fast_cash_pick: Halve — but run it as a hand-sold contingency SERVICE (an AI cost-reduction engagement: ingest a startup's last 30 days of agent/API logs, replay, route high-volume sub-tasks to distilled/open-weight students behind a quality gate, charge a flat fee plus a share of verified savings), NOT the SaaS the memo describes. It is the only idea with pain acute enough (the entire 2026 narrative: Uber blew its budget in 4 months, per-seat caps everywhere), buyers in the founder's actual reachable network (startups), and a contingency structure that can realistically clear ~$25k in eight weeks.
verdict.durable_pick: Veritas Assurance — the neutral, independent AI assurance/verification company, reached by expanding Halve's verification muscle rather than its cost product. It is the one bet whose structure satisfies the brief's strongest bulldozer-safe criteria simultaneously: a neutral cross-vendor position a lab/hyperscaler is structurally conflicted out of, plus a professional liability/licensure moat, plus a proprietary audit/outcome data flywheel. Caveat the committee must accept: the specific LL144 wedge is theater and the durable bet only clears the buyer's trust bar with a credentialed assurance co-founder and a 2-3 year dataset build.
verdict.two_speed_plan: The fast leg is Halve-as-service: contingency AI cost-reduction for startups in the founder's network. This does three things at once — (1) banks cash to buy runway (target ~$25k in 8 weeks, then $25k/mo on retainers + savings-share), (2) puts the founder INSIDE customers' production AI pipelines with trust and reference logos, and (3) the core technical act of the cost work — replaying logged frontier outputs and quality-gating a cheaper model to prove behavioral equivalence — is literally a verification/eval engine. That equivalence-verification capability is the bridge: 'prove the cheap model matches the expensive one' becomes 'independently verify/attest that an AI system behaves and complies.' As EU AI Act high-risk obligations phase in (Aug 2026) and AI-liability insurers demand compliance-grade auditability, you pivot the same relationships and the same engine into Veritas — neutral AI assurance, the position labs and hyperscalers cannot occupy because they are conflicted out of attesting their own systems. Each cost engagement also deposits permissioned quality/outcome data that seeds the assurance flywheel. The fast leg funds the durable leg with money, and feeds it with both a verification engine and a proprietary dataset.
verdict.build_a_model_answer: Mostly NONE — with one narrow, brief-sanctioned exception. Fast leg (Halve cost work): build NOTHING. Use frontier-parity open weights off the shelf (Qwen3.5, DeepSeek V4, gpt-oss) as students plus the FREE distillation toggles labs already ship (OpenAI store:true, Azure Stored Completions) to deliver savings. Building a model here is exactly why Halve-as-SaaS dies — the model is not the moat; the hand-sold outcome and the relationship are. Durable leg (Veritas assurance): the moat is neutrality + liability/licensure + a proprietary audit-outcome dataset, none of which is a model. A foundation model is off the table (open weights are at parity under permissive licenses) and generic finetuning-as-a-service is on the kill list. The ONE place a model becomes the moat — and only after the data exists — is a per-customer FINETUNE/DISTILL on each client's proprietary logs and audit outcomes, used as the equivalence/on-policy verification classifier. That is the brief's permitted case: the model itself is defensible solely because it is trained on proprietary, permissioned data you alone hold via the assurance relationship. So: NONE for capability today; per-customer finetune later, as a data flywheel, never as a from-scratch build.
eight_week_plan: Week 1: From the founder's startup network, pick 2 companies burning >$20k/mo on frontier APIs/agents. Offer a free one-week cost audit of their last 30 days of logs and invoices. Stand up a minimal harness — log ingest, replay against logged frontier outputs, a cost model — using off-the-shelf open-weight students plus the free OpenAI/Azure distillation toggles. No product, just a script.

Week 2: Deliver both audits showing 30-50% savings on 2-3 high-volume task types, with a replay-verified quality gate so the customer trusts it. Convert to paid engagements priced as a flat fee plus a share of verified savings; require a 50% upfront deposit and an LOI. First cash in.

Week 3: Execute engagement #1 — route the safe sub-tasks to distilled/open-weight students behind the quality gate, leaving hard reasoning on frontier. Document savings vs the logged baseline. Ask for two referrals while the result is fresh.

Week 4: Execute engagement #2 and collect its deposit. Productize the audit into a repeatable 3-day checklist so each new engagement costs you ~15 hours, not 60. Begin logging (with written consent) the equivalence/quality-gate outcomes — this is the seed of the durable assurance dataset.
Week 5: Close 2 referral engagements. Introduce a monthly retainer ($3-5k) plus ongoing savings-share so revenue becomes recurring, not one-shot. Running total in deposits + fees should be ~$12-15k.

Week 6: Verify cumulative savings on the first two engagements and invoice the back-half. Land 1 more engagement. Publish 2 short, numbers-backed case studies (named if permitted) to make the next sales cycle warm instead of cold.

Week 7: Push retainers live and collect. Target mix: 3-4 active engagements at ~$5-8k effective each. Cross the ~$25k cleared mark. Start one explicit governance conversation with the most trusting customer ('you'll need independent assurance for the EU AI Act in August').

Week 8: Reconcile cash to confirm ~$25k cleared. Bank the assets that matter more than the cash: 2 case studies, a working verification/quality-gate engine, and a permissioned outcome dataset. Write the one-page thesis for the Veritas durable pivot and start the search for a credentialed assurance co-founder.
verdict.what_to_shelve: Do NOT build the Halve SaaS platform, a gateway/router, a vector DB, a generic eval/observability dashboard, a standalone memory layer, or any foundation model — every one is on the stated kill list and OpenAI/Azure already ship the productized distillation toggle for free. Shelve the LL144 bias-audit wedge as a standalone business (zero enforcement, Bureau Veritas owns the name, the per-system revenue multiplier collapses in the vendor channel) — keep only the EU AI Act Aug-2026 assurance angle as the durable seed. Do not head-butt entrenched incumbents who already shipped the exact product: Skyflow (Cleanroom), Endor/Snyk (Clearweight), Scale/Lightwheel (Tendon), Deltek (Allowable), PointClickCare (Caseworth), Yardi (Recert), Foundation/LCPtracker (Draw), Tropic/Vendr (CompuQuote), WiseTech/Gaia (Declarant). Abandon Attestable entirely — the core product is legally impossible (outsourcing Rule 11 certification is UPL). And resist turning the fast leg into a permanent consulting body shop; treat it strictly as runway-and-data for the durable pivot.
verdict.why_now: Two clocks are aligned in 2026, which is the entire reason a two-speed works. The fast clock: AI spend is exploding and visibly out of control (Uber blew its full 2026 AI budget in four months, per-seat $1,500-2,000 caps are now standard), so cost-reduction is screaming, immediate, contingency-friendly pain — cash is available this quarter. The durable clock: EU AI Act high-risk obligations phase in August 2026 into a notified-body shortage, and AI-liability insurers (Armilla, Lloyd's) now require compliance-grade auditability as baseline — manufacturing genuine 2026-2027 demand for independent assurance exactly as the cost cash arrives. Critically, independent assurance is a position labs and hyperscalers are structurally conflicted out of (you cannot credibly attest your own system), so for once the bulldozer cannot simply roll over you from above. The window is the 12-18 months before TIC giants and Big 4 fully staff specialized AI-assurance practices.
verdict.the_hard_truth: All sixteen ideas were red-teamed to KILL, and the committee should say so plainly: none has the moat the brief demands, and most are either a feature an incumbent already shipped or a thin layer wedged between two products the buyer already owns. The fast leg (cost-reduction service) is real money but a commodity consulting business with no durability — the day you stop hand-selling it evaporates, and OpenAI's free distillation toggle already is the productized version. The durable assurance bet is the only structurally sound option, yet you — a solo, non-credentialed founder — are precisely not who CISOs and regulators trust to attest AI systems, Bureau Veritas already owns the brand and the accreditation, and the data/trust flywheel takes 2-3 years you must survive. The candid recommendation: take the Halve cash to buy runway and learning, but hold the durable pick as a hypothesis to validate, not a conviction — and unless you can recruit a credentialed assurance co-founder, the durable leg will not clear the trust bar, in which case the disciplined move is to greenfield a genuinely novel wedge rather than force any of these pre-bulldozed ideas.
